title: How Should Pre-Trained Language Models Be Fine-Tuned Towards Adversarial Robustness?

structured.summary: This paper proposes Robust Informative Fine-Tuning (RIFT), an information-theoretic adversarial fine-tuning method for pre-trained language models that mitigates catastrophic forgetting by maximizing the conditional mutual information between the fine-tuned and pre-trained model features given the label. Empirical results on sentiment analysis and natural language inference tasks demonstrate that RIFT improves adversarial robustness against synonym substitution attacks, outperforming existing baselines while maintaining or improving clean accuracy.

strengths.point: Addresses catastrophic forgetting in adversarial fine-tuning of PLMs via an information-theoretic framework
strengths.evidence: The method maximizes conditional mutual information I(S; T | Y) to preserve pre-trained robust features aligned within class manifolds, which is novel and conceptually well-motivated.

strengths.point: Consistent empirical improvements on multiple datasets, models, and attack types
strengths.evidence: RIFT outperforms strong baselines on IMDB and SNLI under Genetic and PWWS synonym substitution attacks, with up to 2.9% robust accuracy gains.

weaknesses.point: Lacks comparison or discussion of related prior work using mutual information or contrastive representation alignment
weaknesses.evidence: No comparison to InfoMin principle, contrastive knowledge distillation, or other MI-based robustness approaches, weakening novelty claims.

weaknesses.point: Limited evaluation scope and robustness assessment
weaknesses.evidence: Experiments only on two classification tasks with simple labels, and robustness tested solely on synonym substitution attacks; other NLP tasks and adversarial methods are unexplored.

weaknesses.point: Insufficient ablation and analysis of critical hyperparameters and training details
weaknesses.evidence: No ablation on InfoNCE temperature, negative sampling, or α trade-off beyond narrow ranges; unclear if pre-trained extractor is frozen; no computational cost or stability analysis.

weaknesses.point: Missing theoretical and quantitative analyses supporting key claims
weaknesses.evidence: Limited intuitive explanation for why conditioning on labels improves alignment; no quantitative cluster quality metrics or MI estimator bias discussion; no layer-wise feature alignment analysis.

weaknesses.point: Reproducibility hindered by missing implementation details and lack of multiple runs
weaknesses.evidence: Key hyperparameters, optimizer settings, and random seed variance are not fully specified or reported.

questions.question: Does conditioning on labels in mutual information maximization provide unique benefits over unconditional MI or other contrastive objectives?
questions.motivation: Clarifying this would strengthen theoretical understanding and justify the core design choice of RIFT.

questions.question: How does RIFT perform on other NLP tasks beyond classification and under diverse adversarial attacks such as paraphrasing or character-level perturbations?
questions.motivation: Evaluating generalizability and broader robustness is important for practical adoption.

questions.question: What is the computational overhead of RIFT compared to standard adversarial fine-tuning, and how sensitive are results to hyperparameter choices like temperature and α?
questions.motivation: Understanding efficiency and robustness to design choices is crucial for real-world deployment.

critique_points.point_001: Lacks comparison or discussion of related prior work using mutual information or contrastive representation alignment. No comparison to InfoMin principle, contrastive knowledge distillation, or other MI-based robustness approaches, weakening novelty claims
critique_points.point_002: Limited evaluation scope and robustness assessment. Experiments only on two classification tasks with simple labels, and robustness tested solely on synonym substitution attacks; other NLP tasks and adversarial methods are unexplored
critique_points.point_003: Insufficient ablation and analysis of critical hyperparameters and training details. No ablation on InfoNCE temperature, negative sampling, or α trade-off beyond narrow ranges; unclear if pre-trained extractor is frozen; no computational cost or stability analysis
critique_points.point_004: Missing theoretical and quantitative analyses supporting key claims. Limited intuitive explanation for why conditioning on labels improves alignment; no quantitative cluster quality metrics or MI estimator bias discussion; no layer-wise feature alignment analysis
critique_points.point_005: Reproducibility hindered by missing implementation details and lack of multiple runs. Key hyperparameters, optimizer settings, and random seed variance are not fully specified or reported
